title: Fermented Chewy Greek Tamarind-paste-makham Borek
description: A lacto-vegetarian-friendly take on traditional Greek borek, featuring chewy dough and a tangy fermented tamarind-paste-makham filling. Spherified for added texture.
instructions: Combine warm milk and yeast in a small bowl. Let sit for 5 minutes until frothy.
In a large mixing bowl, combine flour, salt, and sesame oil. Gradually add in yeast mixture and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl and let rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Preheat a non-stick pan or griddle to 37586F.
Divide the dough into 6-8 equal pieces. Roll each piece into a ball and then flatten slightly into a disk shape.
In a small bowl, mix together tamarind paste and makham paste. Spoon a small amount onto the center of each dough disk.
Fold the dough over the filling to form a triangle and press edges to seal.
In a large pot of boiling water, add spherification powder. Gently add dough triangles to the water and cook for 3-5 minutes, or until puffed and chewy.
Drain and serve hot with a dollop of sour cream on top.
Use a food processor to mix and aerate sour cream until light and fluffy, if desired.
